behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-to-text model output (speaker diarization quality/labels), which a stub would mask. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on a single model output property, "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels," not a required multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting audio sample through the production STT API with diarization enabled; assert transcript segments contain stable speaker labels and that labels are preserved in the handoff payload consumed by summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text/objects into a structured schema, and can fail within one local boundary without real I/O. Rejected Integration+: text says "Transcript parsing extracts..." and does not require OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge phrasing), call the parsing function and assert exact assignee/task fields in the produced summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether LLM output covers assigned attendees; a deterministic mock summary would always hide omission failures. Rejected Workflow: although the product pipeline has multiple stages, this behavior can be validated against one real summarization decision from a fixed transcript.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a real transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarization model; assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one returned action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The phrase "identified decisions" disqualifies model-quality dependence: this is deterministic routing/mapping logic after identification. Rejected Integration+: no required real infra or model output dependence in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Pass an in-memory structure of already-identified decisions into the routing/mapping function; assert outputs populate `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is about whether the real LLM places decision content in the correct section; stubs cannot validate model judgment/formatting behavior. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be tested with a fixed transcript and one real summarization step, without requiring multi-agentic orchestration checks.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real transcript samples containing explicit decisions to the live summarization model; assert each expected decision appears in `decisions` and is not only present in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential segmented processing plus merge coherence, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence (segment transcribe/summarize then combined output). Rejected lower levels because they cannot expose cross-step degradation and merge-quality failures across real model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run a >60-minute real recording through the live pipeline; verify segment boundaries and ordered processing events, then assert merged output is a single structured summary with consistent cross-segment action items/decisions and no dropped segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration over multiple real model-dependent stages and external interfaces; failures emerge from chained handoffs, retries, and state transitions across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run with a real meeting file: upload, live STT, live LLM summarization, and final structured delivery. Assert completion status, schema-valid output, and zero manual recovery actions.
